figures.caption: Figure 8: Ensembling failure (example 2). Ensemble averaging degrades grounding quality by favoring prompt-agnostic regions over semantically specific ones.
Figure 9: Text embedding cosine similarity vs. grounding disagreement for 15 prompt pairs. The moderate correlation (𝑟 = −0.58) confirms that text proximity alone cannot predict grounding stability.
Figure 4: Pairwise prompt disagreement rate. Fraction of images where two prompts select different regions.
Figure 5: PCAprojection of CLIP similarity score vectorswith prompt-specific selections highlighted. Distinct directional lobes indicate structured variability.
Figure 6: Mean vs. variance of CLIP similarity scores. High similarity does not imply prompt stability.
